features.name: Features
features.description: User-facing component opt-in/out system and configuration-time requirements.

content.summary: Parent node for feature preferences and configuration-time requirements.
content.full: Parent node for feature preferences and configuration-time requirements. See `features/preferences`.

features/preferences.name: Feature Preferences
features/preferences.description: How to check feature preferences before recommending or using a component, and how the requirements system differs from runtime health checks.
features/preferences.trigger: Before recommending, probing, or diagnosing a feature; whenever a user asks 'why isn't X working?'

content.summary: Check features.<name>.wanted in config.local.yaml before recommending or diagnosing a feature. wanted:false means don't probe; point at /wb-setup preferences.
content.full:
Users can opt in or out of components via `features:` in `config.local.yaml`. The setup wizard (`/wb-setup`) manages these preferences interactively.

## Check preferences before acting

**Before recommending or using a feature, check preferences:**

- If `wanted: false` — do **not** suggest, probe, or diagnose it. If the user asks "why isn't X working?", mention they opted out and point them at `/wb-setup preferences` to re-enable.
- If `wanted: true` or `wanted: null` (undecided) — use normally.
- `feature_status` returns preferences + tool availability in one call. Prefer it over probing components individually.

## Requirements system vs health checks

Two adjacent systems, distinct purposes:

- **Requirements** (`work_buddy/health/requirements.py`) — configuration-time checks that validate hidden assumptions: vault sections exist, required plugins are enabled, config keys are set. Run by the setup wizard; failures come with fix instructions.
- **Health checks** — runtime checks that a service is actually up and responding.

Rule of thumb: "is it configured?" → requirements. "is it running?" → health.

## Capabilities

- `setup_wizard` — modes: `status` (overview), `guided` (interactive setup), `diagnose` (deep diagnostic), `preferences` (view/edit).
- `feature_status` — includes `preferences` and `bootstrap_requirements` sections alongside tool-probe results.
